metadata.title: Build a Unit Circle Visualizer with Claude Code

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B09: palette=claude but the outro is 'NikBearBrownOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration: Claude Code builds a unit circle — revealing how every trig value is just a coordinate on a circle of radius one.
beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: This is Liam, in for Bear.

beats.narration: Sine and cosine of an angle: memorizing tables is fragile. The unit circle gives you the values from geometry — no memorization required.
We ask Claude to build a unit circle showing the point at forty-five degrees with the cosine and sine projections.

props.command:
claude "build unit circle visualization:
  radius = 1  center at origin
  point at theta = 45 degrees
  show cos(theta) and sin(theta) legs
  label exact values sqrt(2)/2"

beats.narration: Claude computes the coordinates. Cosine of forty-five degrees equals root two over two. Sine equals the same. The point on the circle is at root two over two, root two over two.

props.code:
# unit_circle.py — Claude Code output
import math
R = 1.0
theta = math.pi / 4  # 45 degrees

# point on unit circle
x = R * math.cos(theta)  # sqrt(2)/2 ≈ 0.707
y = R * math.sin(theta)  # sqrt(2)/2 ≈ 0.707

print(f'cos(45°) = {x:.4f} = sqrt(2)/2')
print(f'sin(45°) = {y:.4f} = sqrt(2)/2')
print(f'Point: ({x:.3f}, {y:.3f})')

beats.narration: The unit circle at forty-five degrees: the radius line to the point, the cosine leg along the x-axis, the sine leg vertical. Both equal root two over two.
Now Claude generates a table of exact trig values for the standard angles: zero, thirty, forty-five, sixty, ninety, and one hundred eighty degrees.

props.command: claude "generate exact trig value table:\n  angles: 0, pi/6, pi/4, pi/3, pi/2, pi\n  columns: angle, degrees, sin, cos, tan"

beats.narration: The table of exact values: zero through one hundred eighty degrees. Sine and cosine swap at thirty and sixty. Tangent is undefined at ninety because cosine is zero there.
The unit circle gives exact trig values from geometry. Every angle maps to a point — cosine is the x-coordinate, sine is the y-coordinate, tangent is their ratio.
Next: compound interest calculator — building the formula that makes money grow continuously.
Like and subscribe for more math with Claude Code.
